name: Databricks to Fabric Migration
description: Port Databricks notebooks and jobs to Microsoft Fabric — translate dbutils/DBFS/Unity Catalog references, load data into a Lakehouse, deploy, and validate.
prerequisites: Exported Databricks notebooks (.py/.ipynb) and job definitions
Target Fabric workspace with F2+ capacity

steps.description: Load the translation table (dbutils -> notebookutils, DBFS -> OneLake, Unity Catalog -> Lakehouse).
Create target Lakehouse. Map Unity Catalog catalog.schema.table -> lakehouse Tables/ (Delta is compatible).
Migrate data files from DBFS/S3/ADLS into OneLake. Delta tables copy directly (same format).

Translate each notebook: dbutils.fs -> notebookutils.fs, dbutils.secrets -> notebookutils.credentials, spark.table('cat.sch.tbl') -> spark.read of lakehouse Tables/, remove %run magic (use notebookutils.notebook.run or shared code).

tips: Databricks Jobs -> Fabric Data Pipelines or job schedules (fabio job-scheduler create-schedule).
Cluster configs do not migrate — Fabric manages Spark compute.
Delta tables are cross-compatible; often just re-point the path rather than re-write data.
Route the overall effort with 'fabio context persona migration-engineer'.
